narrative.headline: Service brakes lead 2018 Nissan Pathfinder complaints
narrative.summary: Of 76 owner complaints filed with NHTSA, service brakes are the most reported area, with 20 reports (26%). The electrical system is next at 13 reports (17%), followed by the engine at 12 reports (16%) and the power train at 11 reports (14%). Most reports describe no crash, but the service brakes and the power train each show 1 crash, and the power train also has 1 injury. Buyers should pay close attention to the braking and drivetrain systems.

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: The most reported area: 20 reports, or 26% of all complaints, including 1 crash. Test the brakes carefully before buying.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: The second most common concern: 13 reports (17% of complaints), with no crashes or fires reported. Check that the electronics work as expected.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: 12 reports (16% of complaints), with no crashes or fires reported. Have the engine inspected before buying.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: 11 reports (14% of complaints), tied to 1 crash and 1 injury. Take any transmission or drivetrain symptoms seriously on a test drive.

issues.title: Vehicle Speed Control
issues.blurb: 9 reports (12% of complaints), with no crashes or fires reported. Verify the cruise control and speed systems on a test drive.
